title: Rewrite all 16 bare empty states with actionable hints and input examples
description:
ONBOARDING AUDIT: 16 of 29 empty states show only 'X is empty' with zero guidance. R-Tree (:3351) and G-Counter (:4179) already nail the pattern: 'R-Tree is empty. Insert points like "30,50"'. Apply this pattern to all 16 bare states.

Rewrite each to: '{DS name} is empty. {Action verb} {example input format}.'

Examples:
- BST: 'BST is empty. Type a number (e.g., 42) and click Insert to build your tree.'
- Heap: 'Heap is empty. Insert numbers to see the heap property in action.'
- Bloom Filter: 'Bloom filter has 0 items. Insert strings like "alice" to see bits set.'
- Fenwick Tree: 'Fenwick Tree is empty. Enter index and delta (e.g., 3, +5) to update.'

This is the #1 onboarding fix — 2-3 hour task, no architecture changes, addresses beginner and mobile failure modes.

Evidence: Onboarding audit scored 16 empty states at 1/5. R-Tree and G-Counter scored 5/5.
acceptanceCriteria: All 16 bare 'X is empty' states replaced with actionable hints
Each hint includes the exact input format with example values
Hints use action verbs (Insert, Type, Enter) not passive descriptions
R-Tree and G-Counter quality level (5/5) applied everywhere
Trie hint updated: 'Insert words like "cat", "car", "card"'
Merkle hint updated: 'Build from comma-separated data like "A,B,C,D"'

title: Add visible labels to DSControls inputs — not just placeholder text
description:
ONBOARDING AUDIT: The value and index inputs at DataStructuresModule.tsx:2572-2593 have only placeholder text ('value', 'index', 'key / element'). Placeholders disappear when the user starts typing. There are no visible <label> elements and no aria-labels.

Add:
1. Visible labels above or beside each input ('Value:', 'Index:', 'Key:')
2. aria-label attributes for screen readers
3. Better placeholders with examples: 'e.g., 42' instead of 'value'
4. Context-sensitive labels that change per DS (Array shows 'Value' + 'Index', Hash shows 'Key' + 'Value', Trie shows 'Word')

Evidence: Onboarding audit input discoverability scored 3/10.
acceptanceCriteria: Visible labels above each input field
Labels change based on active DS context
Placeholders include example values (e.g., 42)
aria-label present on all inputs
Labels persist when input has focus (not placeholder-only)

title: Add DS-specific step to global onboarding tour
description:
ONBOARDING AUDIT: The 5-step OnboardingOverlay at onboarding-overlay.tsx has ZERO DS-specific content. All steps reference System Design concepts (Drag Components, Connect Nodes, Run Simulation). A user entering the DS module sees an irrelevant tour.

Add a 6th step (or conditional DS-specific steps) that targets the DS sidebar and explains: 'Explore 34 data structures — from Arrays to B-Trees. Click any structure to visualize it, then use Insert/Delete/Search to see it in action.'

Alternatively: make the tour context-aware — detect which module is active and show module-specific steps.

Evidence: Onboarding audit scored 0/10 for 'global tour covers DS module'.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding tour includes at least 1 DS-specific step
DS step points to the sidebar structure list
DS step explains: what structures are available, how to interact
Step only appears when DS module is active (or is a universal overview step)
Tour still skippable and not longer than 6-7 steps total

title: Add tooltips to all DS control buttons explaining what they do
description:
ONBOARDING AUDIT: Insert, Delete, Search, Random, Reset, Step buttons have ZERO tooltips. A beginner doesn't know what 'Step' does or that 'Random' generates example data.

Add title attributes or tooltip components:
- Insert: 'Add a new element to the data structure'
- Delete: 'Remove an element by value or index'
- Search: 'Find an element and highlight the search path'
- Random: 'Generate random example data to explore'
- Reset: 'Clear all data and start fresh'
- Step: 'Advance to the next step in the operation trace'

ContextualTooltips component exists but is never rendered for DS module — either wire it or use simple title attributes.

Evidence: Onboarding audit help system inventory — 0 tooltips on any DS control.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6+ control buttons have visible tooltips on hover
Tooltip text explains what the button does in plain English
Tooltips work on both desktop (hover) and mobile (long-press)
Tooltips don't obstruct other controls

title: Add one-click demo button — 'Watch BST Insert →' in empty states
description:
ONBOARDING AUDIT: The biggest gap for beginners is the 35-60 second time-to-first-success. Empty states should have a CTA button that auto-runs a demo.

For key DS empty states, add a button:
- BST empty: 'Watch BST Insert →' → auto-inserts [25, 10, 42, 5, 15, 35, 50] one by one with animation
- Hash Table: 'See Hash Table in Action →' → inserts 3 key-value pairs
- Heap: 'Build a Heap →' → inserts 7 random values

This is the 'Chrome Music Lab' pattern — one click to see something magical happen. Reduces time-to-first-success from 35s to 3s for beginners.

Evidence: Onboarding audit — beginner time-to-aha is 35-60s (accidental). Target: <10s.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 DS have a 'Watch X →' demo button in their empty state
Clicking demo auto-populates with educational example data
Demo uses handleRandom or a curated insertion sequence
Demo button is prominent (primary color, centered in empty state)
After demo, user can interact with the populated DS normally
